creator: Julie Bort
title: (No AI) Yupp shuts down after raising $33M from a16z crypto’s Chris Dixon
dc:creator: Julie Bort
content: Less than a year after launching, with checks from some of the biggest names in Silicon Valley, crowdsourced AI model feedback startup Yupp is closing its business, the company said Tuesday.
contentSnippet: Less than a year after launching, with checks from some of the biggest names in Silicon Valley, crowdsourced AI model feedback startup Yupp is closing its business, the company said Tuesday.

creator: Lauren Forristal
title: (No AI) Alexa+ gets new food ordering experiences with Uber Eats and Grubhub
dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
content: You can now order from Uber Eats and Grubhub using Alexa+, an experience Amazon says will be similar to chatting with a waiter at a restaurant or placing an order at a drive-thru.
contentSnippet: You can now order from Uber Eats and Grubhub using Alexa+, an experience Amazon says will be similar to chatting with a waiter at a restaurant or placing an order at a drive-thru.

title: (No AI) The Galaxy S26’s photo app can sloppify your memories
author: Allison Johnson
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/ai-label-17.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Love to see the BACKSST BOYS at the Sphere.

The Google Pixel 9 walked so that the Samsung Galaxy S26 could run.

Google introduced AI editing tools to Photos slowly. It started with [changes to the background](https://www.theverge.com/2023/5/10/23716165/google-photos-ai-magic-editor-transform-pixel-io) - make the sky more blue, or remove crowds of tourists. Things got weird once the company added natural language requests and let you ask for basically any change. There were some guardrails, but in many cases [it was easy to prompt your way around them](https://www.theverge.com/2024/8/21/24224084/google-pixel-9-reimagine-ai-photos) into creating a potentially harmful image of something that never happened - helicopter crashes, smoking bombs on street corners, that kind of thing.

That's the world Samsung's updated Photo Assist steps into. At Unpacked in Febru …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/904176/samsung-galaxy-s26-ai-photo-assist-slop)

Things got weird once the company added natural language requests and let you ask for basically any change. There were some guardrails, but in many cases it was easy to prompt your way around them into creating a potentially harmful image of something that never happened - helicopter crashes, smoking bombs on street corners, that kind of thing. 
That's the world Samsung's updated Photo Assist steps into. At Unpacked in Febru …

title: (No AI) Art schools are being torn apart by AI
author: Jess Weatherbed
content:
![A wooden canvas illustrated on a green background.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/268425_How_art_schools_are_teaching_AI__CVirginia.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

The curriculum at creative institutions is evolving to handle gen AI tools, and a lot of people aren’t happy about it. | Image by Cath Virginia / The Verge

When my baby brother, a 3D modelling and animation student, talks to me about his projects and studies, the pride I usually feel is becoming increasingly tainted by a growing sense of dread. As a creative professional and former design student myself, I understand all too well how fierce the competition for postgraduate jobs will be, but his future is being threatened by something that barely even existed during my own time in higher education: generative AI.

College students are feeling that fear as well. Earlier this year, in a small protest at CalArts, posters that requested the help of AI artists for a thesis were reportedly [altered wit …](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7cbpu7IQEts&t)

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/903954/art-schools-generative-ai-education-creative-jobs)

title: (No AI) Protecting people from harmful manipulation

creator: Boone Ashworth
title: (No AI) AI Has Flooded All the Weather Apps

content: Weather forecasting has gotten a big boost from machine learning. How that translates into what users see can vary.

creator: Caroline Haskins
title: (No AI) The IRS Wants Smarter Audits. Palantir Could Help Decide Who Gets Flagged
dc:creator: Caroline Haskins
content: Documents show the tax agency is testing a Palantir tool to surface “highest-value” audit and investigation targets from a maze of legacy systems.
contentSnippet: Documents show the tax agency is testing a Palantir tool to surface “highest-value” audit and investigation targets from a maze of legacy systems.
